pattern: Carrying the running min and max seen along the path from the root lets each node compare itself against its extreme ancestor in one pass, no pairwise search needed.
hints: The biggest |a.val - b.val| for an ancestor a of b uses, for each leaf-reaching path, the largest and smallest ancestor values encountered.
Thread the minimum and maximum ancestor value down through the recursion as you descend.
At each node, the candidate answer is the gap between the current node's value and the path's min and max so far; update those before recursing into the children.

edge_inputs.description: balanced tree with the extreme pair near the root
skewed chain where min and max bookend a path
equal values along one branch contribute zero
large gap is on the right branch only
best gap appears deep, not at the root

stress.description: random tree with values in range to exercise min/max threading
stress.note: at least two real nodes so an ancestor pair always exists
